events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess block/brick laying residential, observed bond pattern broken, rough-complete, reference TMS 402/602. Need estimate measurable condition and state why out of tolerance. Decision fail if dangerous or non-compliant. Broken bond pattern in masonry is non-compliant? Need determine tolerance. Bond pattern: running bond required for masonry walls, minimum 25% overlap for hollow unit masonry? TMS 602 likely requires head joints in successive courses offset at least 1/4 unit length, or running bond with overlap not less than 2 in? For brick, bond pattern broken means not maintaining proper overlap. This weakens wall, reduces stability, non-compliant. So decision fail, risk medium/high? Residential non-structural? Could be high if structural wall. But generic risk medium. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 80. value maybe estimate overlap deficiency e.g., 0 in overlap or <2 in. But task says estimate or interpret measurable condition. We can set value to estimated lap e.g., 0.0 inches or 1.0 inch (below required 2 in). Need include rationale and references. Use TMS 402/602. Need ensure JSON valid. No prose outside.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "high",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 80,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.85,
  "event_time": 0,
  "rate": 0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": ["Bond pattern is broken; head joints in successive courses are not properly lapped/overlapped.", "Masonry units are stacked with vertical or near-vertical joints aligned, creating a continuous plane of weakness."],
  "actions": ["Remove and rebuild the affected courses to restore proper running bond and required head-joint overlap.", "Verify bond pattern and overlap with the approved plans and TMS 602 before continuing."],
  "rationale": "TMS 602 requires masonry units to be laid in running bond with head joints in successive courses offset sufficiently (typically not less than one-quarter the unit length or as specified) to develop wall strength and stability. A visibly broken bond pattern with little or no overlap is out of tolerance, structurally weakens the wall, and is non-compliant.",
  "references": ["TMS 402/602"]
}
